deep_dive.title: Evolutionary Bilevel Reward Shaping for Generalization in Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) often suffers from performance degradation when deployed in environments that differ from those encountered during training. Existing techniques such as domain randomization (DR) mitigate this, but require access to diverse training environments and full trajectory observability, assumptions that fail in privacy-preserving or restricted scenarios where only scalar performance metrics are available. We propose Generalization via Evolutionary Reward Shaping (GERS), a bilevel optimization approach to improve generalization on unseen test environments using only scalar feedback from validation environments. At the lower level, an RL agent guided via a reward function shaped by the upper level learns a policy on a limited set of training environments with accessible trajectory data; at the upper level, CMA-ES optimizes the reward shaping parameters to maximize the cumulative unshaped reward on separate validation environments for which trajectory access is unavailable. Results on continuous control tasks indicate that GERS outperforms the standard RL baseline on unseen test environments. GERS performance is comparable to DR, despite DR treating the combined set of training and validation environments of GERS as a single training set that requires trajectory access, whereas GERS cannot access validation trajectories. These results confirm that GERS effectively enhances generalization under restricted data access constraints.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning generalization via bilevel reward shaping
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于双层奖励塑形的强化学习泛化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于双层奖励塑形的强化学习泛化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于双层奖励塑形的强化学习泛化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GERS, a bilevel optimization approach for RL generalization that uses only scalar feedback from validation environments.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对强化学习在未知环境泛化性能退化的问题，提出基于演化双层奖励塑形的方法GERS，仅利用验证环境的标量反馈，通过上层优化奖励函数、下层训练策略，在隐私受限场景下实现有效泛化。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对强化学习在未知环境泛化性能退化的问题，提出基于演化双层奖励塑形的方法GERS，仅利用验证环境的标量反馈，通过上层优化奖励函数、下层训练策略，在隐私受限场景下实现有效泛化。

deep_dive.title: HOLO-MPPI: Multi-Scenario Motion Planning via Hierarchical Policy Optimization
deep_dive.abstract: Robots deployed in the real world must plan motions across diverse scenarios without per-scenario retuning. End-to-end reinforcement learning (RL) can generalize across scenarios but often becomes brittle under distribution shift, reward misspecification, and stochastic interactions. Model predictive path integral (MPPI) control enables strong real-time refinement without gradients, but its performance depends on a well-shaped sampling prior, while manually designing the priors does not scale to multi-scenario deployment. We present HOLO-MPPI (High-level Offline, Low-level Online MPPI), a multi-scenario motion planning framework that combines high-level policy learning with low-level stochastic optimal control. Offline, we learn a high-level policy that proposes scenario-robust plans in an abstract action space, with a learned world model for online rollout. Online, the policy serves as a data-driven prior generator that parameterizes MPPI's sampling distribution conditioned on the current observation and goal. MPPI then optimizes low-level control sequences around this prior in real time to adapt to local disturbances. We instantiate HOLO-MPPI in autonomous driving by designing an effective high-level action space and tailored model architectures. Our evaluation across diverse driving scenarios shows that HOLO-MPPI improves upon MPPI and end-to-end RL baselines while maintaining real-time control.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: multi-scenario motion planning with hierarchical RL and MPPI
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 结合分层强化学习和MPPI的多场景运动规划
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 结合分层强化学习和MPPI的多场景运动规划
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 结合分层强化学习和MPPI的多场景运动规划
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: HOLO-MPPI combines offline high-level policy learning with online MPPI control for multi-scenario robot motion planning, addressing brittleness of end-to-end RL.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出HOLO-MPPI框架，解决端到端强化学习在分布变化下脆弱性的问题。通过离线学习高层策略作为MPPI的采样先验，在线实时优化，实现多场景运动规划。实验表明该方法在多种机器人导航任务中优于纯RL和纯MPPI基线。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出HOLO-MPPI框架，解决端到端强化学习在分布变化下脆弱性的问题。通过离线学习高层策略作为MPPI的采样先验，在线实时优化，实现多场景运动规划。实验表明该方法在多种机器人导航任务中优于纯RL和纯MPPI基线。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning for embodied robot navigation

deep_dive.title: BRICKS-WM: Building Reusability via Interface Composition Kinetics for Structured World Models
deep_dive.abstract: Model-based Reinforcement Learning (MBRL) has achieved remarkable success in continuous control by leveraging latent world models. However, prevailing approaches typically rely on monolithic latent dynamics, entangling environment dynamics into a coupled process. This coupling severely limits reusability: altering the agent necessitates retraining the entire world from scratch, even if the environment remains constant. To address this, we introduce BRICKS-WM (Building Reusability via Interface Composition Kinetics for Structured World Models), a framework for the modular assembly of structured world models. Driven by the insight that the physical world is composed of independent entities, we posit that global dynamics can be modeled as a composition of distinct dynamical modules interacting via latent interfaces. As a minimal instantiation, we factorize the latent state space into an actuated Agent module and an external Background module, bridged by a learned latent interface. Unlike prior object-centric methods that prioritize visual segmentation, BRICKS-WM enforces a functional separation in transition dynamics, ensuring that background dynamics remains agnostic to the agent's dynamics. Empirically, BRICKS-WM achieves control performance comparable to strong monolithic baselines when trained from scratch, and enables the reuse of frozen background dynamics across agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: model-based reinforcement learning with structured world models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于模型的结构化世界模型强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于模型的结构化世界模型强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于模型的结构化世界模型强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes BRICKS-WM, a framework for modular assembly of structured world models for MBRL, enabling reusability by decoupling dynamics into independent entities.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有模型基强化学习使用单一日志动态，导致重用时需从头训练。本文提出BRICKS-WM，将全局动态分解为独立实体的接口组合，支持模块化组装。通过接口组合动力学，改变智能体时只需替换对应模块。实验表明在连续控制任务中，BRICKS-WM在保持性能的同时显著提高了模块重用性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有模型基强化学习使用单一日志动态，导致重用时需从头训练。本文提出BRICKS-WM，将全局动态分解为独立实体的接口组合，支持模块化组装。通过接口组合动力学，改变智能体时只需替换对应模块。实验表明在连续控制任务中，BRICKS-WM在保持性能的同时显著提高了模块重用性。

quick_skim.title: TopoRetarget: Interaction-Preserving Retargeting for Dexterous Manipulation
quick_skim.abstract: Human hand-object demonstrations provide dense reference motions for training dexterous manipulation reinforcement learning (RL) policies through reference tracking. However, to use such demonstrations for RL policy learning, retargeting must preserve hand pose and task-relevant hand-object contact structure. Otherwise, contact and feasibility artifacts can degrade downstream RL policy performance. We introduce TopoRetarget, an interaction-preserving retargeting framework that uses a single set of parameters across diverse retargeting conditions while maintaining task-relevant hand-object interaction and adapting human demonstrations to dexterous robot hands. The method constructs a sparse interaction graph over hand and object keypoints and optimizes distance-weighted Laplacian deformation with directional consistency, kinematic constraints, and penetration handling. Evaluations show that the generated references improve both interaction fidelity and policy learning: TopoRetarget achieves the best contact precision and alignment over all baselines on the ContactPose Dataset, improves Pen-Spin training success by 40.6 percentage points over the existing baseline methods, and enables zero-shot transfer to Wuji Hand hardware on cube reorientation and pen spinning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reinforcement learning for dexterous manipulation retargeting
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于灵巧操作的强化学习重定向
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于灵巧操作的强化学习重定向
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于灵巧操作的强化学习重定向
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: TopoRetarget preserves hand-object interaction structure during retargeting for dexterous manipulation RL, using sparse interaction graphs to avoid artifacts.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 利用人类演示训练灵巧操作RL策略时，重定向需保持手-物接触结构。TopoRetarget构建稀疏交互图，优化手和物体关键点间的距离，在多种重定向条件下保持交互不变。该方法减少了RL策略训练中的接触伪影和可行性问题，提升了下游策略性能。该工作直接服务于具身机器人中的强化学习策略学习。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 利用人类演示训练灵巧操作RL策略时，重定向需保持手-物接触结构。TopoRetarget构建稀疏交互图，优化手和物体关键点间的距离，在多种重定向条件下保持交互不变。该方法减少了RL策略训练中的接触伪影和可行性问题，提升了下游策略性能。该工作直接服务于具身机器人中的强化学习策略学习。

quick_skim.title: RoboPIN: Grounded Embodied Reasoning via Pinned Chain-of-Thought
quick_skim.abstract: Embodied reasoning requires models to perceive task-relevant objects and spaces in physical environments and maintain consistent visual grounding throughout multi-step reasoning. However, current vision-language models rely on text-only or coordinate-augmented chain-of-thought, where entity references remain implicit and ambiguous. This may cause the reasoning process to decouple from visual evidence, entity references to drift across steps, and a causal disconnection between the reasoning trajectory and the final answer, with these problems further amplified in multi-view scenarios due to cross-view appearance changes. To address these issues, we propose Pinned Chain-of-Thought (\pincot{}), a structured reasoning paradigm that pins every reasoning step to visual evidence. \pincot{} introduces the concept of \reasoninganchor{}, which binds each task-relevant entity to a structured visual anchor with entity name, unique identity, view index, and spatial grounding, enabling consistent entity tracking across reasoning steps and views. We build a fully automated data generation pipeline to construct \dataset{}, a high-quality \pincot{}-formatted reasoning dataset. We then train \method{} through three-stage post-training that progressively injects embodied knowledge, structured reasoning ability, and process-supervised alignment, with rewards that directly constrain both anchor localization and identity consistency during reasoning. On 14 benchmarks covering embodied spatial reasoning, multi-view reasoning, and pointing, \method{} with only 4B parameters consistently outperforms 7B level open-source embodied models, achieving a 12\% average improvement over the strongest 7B baseline, Mimo-Embodied. Further analysis shows that \pincot{} improves grounding accuracy and cross-step identity consistency, validating the effectiveness of process supervision.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: grounded embodied reasoning with pinned chain-of-thought for VLN-like tasks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过固定链式推理实现具身推理中的视觉语言导航
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过固定链式推理实现具身推理中的视觉语言导航
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过固定链式推理实现具身推理中的视觉语言导航
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RoboPIN proposes Pinned Chain-of-Thought to maintain visual grounding in multi-step embodied reasoning, useful for VLN.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有视觉语言模型在具身推理中实体引用漂移和视觉证据脱钩的问题，提出Pinned Chain-of-Thought范式，将每个推理步骤锚定到具体视觉区域，确保一致性。在多个VLN风格数据集上提升了准确率。该工作增强了模型在物理环境中执行多步推理的能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有视觉语言模型在具身推理中实体引用漂移和视觉证据脱钩的问题，提出Pinned Chain-of-Thought范式，将每个推理步骤锚定到具体视觉区域，确保一致性。在多个VLN风格数据集上提升了准确率。该工作增强了模型在物理环境中执行多步推理的能力。

quick_skim.title: VENOM: Versatile Embodied Network for Omni-bodied Motion tracking
quick_skim.abstract: Achieving expert-level expressive full-body motion tracking across multiple humanoids solely from demonstration data remains a challenging and relatively an underexplored problem in humanoid robot learning. Cross-embodiment motion tracking policies are mostly trained by decoupling the control problem into upper and lower body control. This work proposes VENOM, a cross-embodiment full-body motion tracking model for humanoids in simulation. VENOM is a GPT-based motion tracker trained on multiple humanoid data that can track the entire body without the requirement to split into upper and lower body control. We curate a multi-humanoid motion tracking dataset called the VENOM dataset that contains states, actions, and rewards and train VENOM and the baselines on this dataset. In this letter, we evaluate VENOM's performance against baselines and show that we can achieve a stable motion tracker across different humanoids more capable than an MLP trained on multiple humanoid data with supervised learning alone, and also show that despite lack of reward feedback, VENOM closely matches the tracking capability of experts that were trained using asymmetric-actor critic reinforcement learning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cross-embodiment full-body motion tracking with RL
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 跨具身全身运动跟踪，涉及强化学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 跨具身全身运动跟踪，涉及强化学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 跨具身全身运动跟踪，涉及强化学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: VENOM is a GPT-based cross-embodiment full-body motion tracker for humanoids, trained on a multi-humanoid dataset with state-action-reward tuples.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多个人形机器人的全身运动跟踪面临跨具身泛化挑战。VENOM提出基于GPT的模型，无需分离上下半身控制，在仿真中实现全身跟踪。它使用包含状态、动作和奖励的多具身数据集训练。该方法展示了利用演示数据学习全身运动策略的可行性，为具身机器人控制提供了可迁移的框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多个人形机器人的全身运动跟踪面临跨具身泛化挑战。VENOM提出基于GPT的模型，无需分离上下半身控制，在仿真中实现全身跟踪。它使用包含状态、动作和奖励的多具身数据集训练。该方法展示了利用演示数据学习全身运动策略的可行性，为具身机器人控制提供了可迁移的框架。

quick_skim.title: Guava: An Effective and Universal Harness for Embodied Manipulation
quick_skim.abstract: Language models trained on large-scale vision-language data have demonstrated strong potential for embodied agents. Harnessing models through embodied tools use offers a promising alternative to end-to-end vision-language-action systems by combining high-level reasoning with external modules for perception, planning, and control. However, it remains unclear what makes an effective harness for embodied manipulation, and to what extent such a harness can unlock embodied capabilities in a wide range of reasoning models. In this work, we present Guava, a harness framework for embodied tool use developed through systematic exploration of the design space of agent workflows, action spaces, and observation spaces. Our study identifies three key ingredients for effective embodied agents: iterative perception-reasoning-action loops, semantic action abstractions, and multimodal observations. To understand whether these design principles are universal even to small models, we develop an end-to-end training pipeline that distills embodied manipulation capabilities into a 4B open-source model using fewer than 2K trajectories collected entirely in simulation. Experimental results in both simulation and real-world environments show performance comparable to frontier proprietary models while exhibiting strong generalization to unseen objects, novel instructions, and long-horizon tasks. Results suggest that a well-designed harness can serve as a scalable, model-agnostic interface for embodied manipulation, enabling strong emergent embodied capabilities in compact open-source models with minimal training data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: harness framework for embodied manipulation with LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用大语言模型的具身操作工具框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用大语言模型的具身操作工具框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用大语言模型的具身操作工具框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Guava systematically explores design space for embodied tool-use harness, identifying key ingredients that unlock manipulation capabilities in reasoning models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 语言模型在具身智能体中展现出潜力，但如何有效驾驭仍不清楚。本文提出Guava框架，通过系统探索智能体工作流、动作空间和观测空间的设计，识别出三个关键要素，使多种推理模型能通过外部模块完成操作任务。该框架提供了一种替代端到端VLA系统的轻量级方案，为具身机器人的感知-规划-控制集成提供了新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 语言模型在具身智能体中展现出潜力，但如何有效驾驭仍不清楚。本文提出Guava框架，通过系统探索智能体工作流、动作空间和观测空间的设计，识别出三个关键要素，使多种推理模型能通过外部模块完成操作任务。该框架提供了一种替代端到端VLA系统的轻量级方案，为具身机器人的感知-规划-控制集成提供了新工具。

quick_skim.title: DREAM-Chunk: Reactive Action Chunking with Latent World Model
quick_skim.abstract: Action chunking has become a common interface for vision-language-action (VLA) models, enabling low-frequency policy inference to drive high-frequency robot execution. However, once an action chunk is committed, its open-loop execution can be brittle under stochastic dynamics, hardware execution errors, and partial observability. We propose DREAM-Chunk, a test-time scaling method that augments chunking-based policies with a lightweight latent world model, without requiring additional policy fine-tuning. At test time, DREAM-Chunk samples multiple candidate action chunks, rolls out their predicted latent futures, and selects actions from the chunk whose predicted state best matches the observed rollout. In this way, DREAM-Chunk uses additional test-time computation to cover multiple plausible stochastic futures and improve reactivity during long-horizon chunk execution. On the Kinetix benchmark, DREAM-Chunk improves robustness under increasing action noise and benefits from larger candidate sample sizes, especially when demonstrations contain corrective behaviors. We further validate DREAM-Chunk on four manipulation tasks across two robot platforms and two VLA policies under various sources of stochasticity. Across simulation and hardware experiments, DREAM-Chunk improves the robustness of action-chunking policies in stochastic dynamics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: latent world model for VLA action chunking, end-to-end
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 潜世界模型增强VLA动作分块，端到端学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 潜世界模型增强VLA动作分块，端到端学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 潜世界模型增强VLA动作分块，端到端学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes DREAM-Chunk, a test-time scaling method that augments chunking-based policies with a latent world model to improve robustness without fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 动作分块在开环执行中脆弱，受随机性和部分可观测影响。本文提出DREAM-Chunk，在测试时使用轻量潜世界模型对候选动作进行滚动预测，选择最佳匹配动作。无需额外微调即可提升鲁棒性，实验证明在多种机器人任务中效果显著。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 动作分块在开环执行中脆弱，受随机性和部分可观测影响。本文提出DREAM-Chunk，在测试时使用轻量潜世界模型对候选动作进行滚动预测，选择最佳匹配动作。无需额外微调即可提升鲁棒性，实验证明在多种机器人任务中效果显著。
